name: Telegram Mini App screen design spec
description: Capture the package-local visual system, screen map, and interaction rules for the first real TMA shell, expense history, and add-expense screens so future implementation work shares one canonical UI reference.

planned: Used `using-skills` first, then applied the repo-owned `tma-development` skill and the `frontend-design` skill because the work defines TMA-specific screen direction inside `apps/tma`.

Read only the exact TMA/product leaves needed for this design artifact: `docs/TMA.md`, TMA app-structure/native-navigation docs, `docs/product-specs/tma/index.md`, `docs/product-specs/shared/expense-tracking.md`, `docs/product-specs/web/quick-add-experience.md`, and the proposed TMA client-architecture doc.
Reviewed the attached reference screens directly and translated them into one implementation-facing screen spec instead of a vague mood board.
artifacts: Added `apps/tma/DESIGN.md` as the canonical package-local reference for TMA visual tokens, shell rules, page map, page-by-page composition, and performance-first interaction guidance.
Updated `docs/TMA.md` so future TMA work can route directly to the package-local screen spec when the task is visual/page implementation.
Recorded the requested TMA page map, shell rules, and add-expense flow order in one package-local screen spec before runtime implementation began.
verification: Validated `harness/feature_index.json` and `harness/features/feat-087.json` with `python3 -m json.tool`; both parsed successfully.
Ran `./scripts/check_harness_size.sh`, which returned `Harness size checks passed`, and `git diff --check`, which returned clean with no whitespace errors.
Ran final `gitnexus_detect_changes(scope: 'all')`, which returned `LOW` risk with 4 changed symbols, 0 affected processes, and 3 tracked changed files.

notes: Docs-only TMA UI artifact. Later TMA doc alignment removed the old amount-first conflict and kept this screen spec as the package-local visual source of truth.
